attractions.name: Choice City (formerly Choice City Butcher and Deli)
attractions.description: Choice City is a local favorite in Old Town Fort Collins, having evolved from a specialized butcher and deli into a full-service neighborhood restaurant. Known for its high-quality ingredients and extensive craft beer selection, it offers a reimagined menu that blends its classic heritage with modern culinary flair and local partnerships. Choice City Official Website

attractions.name: CooperSmith's Brewpub & Billiards
attractions.description: CooperSmith’s Brewpub & Billiards is Fort Collins' oldest brewpub, serving award-winning craft beer and scratch-made American food since 1989. Located in the heart of Old Town, it features two distinct areas: the main Brewpub for full dinners and patio dining, and the Billiards side for pizza, pool, and board games (Official Website).

attractions.name: Social
attractions.description: Social is a premier underground speakeasy located in the heart of Old Town Fort Collins, offering a refined escape into a bygone era. attractions.name: Old Town Fort Collins
attractions.category: Historic District / Landmark / Shopping & Dining District
attractions.description: Old Town Fort Collins is a vibrant historic district renowned for its beautifully preserved late-19th-century architecture, which served as the primary inspiration for Disneyland's Main Street, USA. The area pulses with history and culture, featuring a pedestrian-friendly plaza, over 20 restored historical buildings, and a dense concentration of unique boutiques, local restaurants, and craft breweries. It serves as the social and cultural heart of the city, hosting numerous festivals, live music events, and community gatherings throughout the year.
attractions.address: 7 Old Town Square, Fort Collins, CO 80524 (Visitor Center)
attractions.region: Downtown Fort Collins / Old Town Triangle
attractions.hours: Public areas are accessible 24/7; Old Town Square events generally occur daily between 5:00 AM and 11:00 PM. Individual business hours typically range from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM for retail, while restaurants and bars remain open until late (10:00 PM – 2:00 AM). The Visitor Center is open Mon–Sat 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Visit Fort Collins, City of Fort Collins
attractions.admission: Free (The district and public square are free to explore; individual businesses and tours may have costs) Visit Fort Collins, Downtown Fort Collins
highlights: Main Street, USA Inspiration: The district's architecture famously inspired the design of Disneyland's iconic entry street.
Old Town Square: A renovated public plaza voted one of the best public squares in the nation, featuring a splash pad, fountain, and fireplace.
Historic Trolley: A restored 1919 trolley (Birney Safety Car 21) that runs from downtown to City Park along Mountain Avenue during summer weekends.
Alley Art: A network of uniquely decorated alleys featuring dynamic art installations, colorful murals, and overhead Tivoli lights.
Art & Food Walks: Monthly events like the First Friday Art Walk and Fort Collins Foodie Walk that showcase local creativity and culinary delights.
Ghost Tours: Popular guided tours exploring the area's haunted history and underground tunnels beneath the historic buildings.
attractions.tips: Park in the Old Town Parking Structure (100 Remington St.) where the first hour is free and Sundays are complimentary. Don't miss the 'Pianos About Town' scattered throughout the district, which are often being painted live by local artists. Explore the hidden alleys like Firehouse Alley for a quieter experience with beautiful art and lighting.
attractions.nearby: Poudre River Whitewater Park, Fort Collins Museum of Discovery, Avery House, Colorado State University (CSU), City Park and Grandview Cemetery.

attractions.name: The 1879 Avery House
attractions.description: The 1879 Avery House is a meticulously preserved Victorian-era home and National Register of Historic Places landmark that honors the legacy of Franklin Avery, the surveyor who designed Fort Collins' iconic wide streets. Visitors can take guided tours through rooms filled with authentic furnishings and artifacts that showcase the daily life of a prominent pioneering family in the late 19th century. The property also features a beautiful carriage house, a historic fountain, and the Margareth Merrill Memorial Gazebo. Poudre Landmarks Foundation, Visit Fort Collins
attractions.address: 328 West Mountain Avenue
attractions.hours: Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Sunday: 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM; Monday–Friday: Closed (Private tours available by appointment). Poudre Landmarks Foundation
attractions.days_open: Saturdays and Sundays (Closed Monday–Friday for regular tours) Poudre Landmarks Foundation
attractions.admission: Adults: $6, Seniors: $5, Children (with parent/guardian): $4, Private Tours: $75 for the first 10 people plus $6 per additional adult or $5 per additional senior. Poudre Landmarks Foundation
highlights: Built in 1879 of local sandstone and listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Wikipedia)
Features a distinctive Queen Anne tower and a carriage house built in 1904. Poudre Landmarks Foundation
Home of Franklin Avery, who was instrumental in developing the city's wide streets and early water projects. Poudre Landmarks Foundation
Collection of authentic turn-of-the-century furnishings, textiles, and household tools. Poudre Landmarks Foundation

attractions.tips: The house is not ADA compliant and requires the use of stairs to tour the upper floors. Allow at least one hour for a full tour, especially on busy weekends. If visiting during the holidays, the house is famously decorated in Victorian Christmas style. Groups of 8 or more should contact the museum in advance. Poudre Landmarks Foundation, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Arapaho and Roosevelt National Forests & Pawnee National Grassland (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.description: The Arapaho and Roosevelt National Forests and Pawnee National Grassland encompass 1.5 million acres of diverse landscapes in north central Colorado, ranging from rolling prairies to alpine tundra. As one of the most visited national forest systems in the United States, it provides critical habitat for wildlife and a vast array of year-round outdoor recreational opportunities. The area is jointly managed to preserve forest health and maintain traditional uses like hunting, fishing, and timber management (USDA Forest Service).
attractions.address: 2150 Centre Ave, Building E, Fort Collins, CO 80526 (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.hours: Forest lands are generally open 24/7; Supervisor's Office is open by appointment Monday-Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Forest lands); Monday - Friday (Supervisor's Office) (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.admission: Free for general forest entry; some recreation sites require a $5 daily fee, a reservation, or a special pass such as the America the Beautiful pass (USDA Forest Service)
highlights: Cache la Poudre River, Colorado's only designated National Wild and Scenic River (Visit Fort Collins)
The Indian Peaks Wilderness, known for its pristine alpine environments and the Arapaho Glacier (Wikipedia)
Brainard Lake Recreation Area, a popular destination for hiking and viewing subalpine scenery (Komoot)
Mount Blue Sky (formerly Mount Evans), which offers one of the highest paved roads in North America (Recreation.gov)
The Pawnee Buttes, iconic geologic formations located in the eastern grassland portion (USDA Forest Service)
Historic sites such as Homestead Meadows, showcasing the area's mining and ranching heritage (Komoot)
attractions.tips: Always check current road and trail conditions before visiting, as high-elevation snow can persist well into the summer. Some popular areas like Brainard Lake and Mount Blue Sky require advanced timed-entry reservations during peak seasons (USDA Forest Service) (Recreation.gov).
attractions.nearby: Rocky Mountain National Park, Horsetooth Reservoir, Lory State Park, Cache la Poudre River National Heritage Area, and Fort Collins Old Town (Visit Fort Collins) (Expedia)

attractions.name: Arthur's Rock Trail
attractions.description: Arthur's Rock Trail is a prominent 1.7-mile (one-way) hiking path located within Lory State Park that leads visitors to a massive granite outcropping. The trail ascends through diverse landscapes, including open meadows and dense ponderosa pine forests, before concluding with a rocky scramble to the 6,780-foot summit. Hikers are rewarded with spectacular panoramic views of Horsetooth Reservoir, the city of Fort Collins, and the surrounding Front Range (Colorado Parks and Wildlife, Visit Fort Collins).
attractions.address: 708 Lodgepole Drive
attractions.hours: Park: 5:00 AM - Sunset daily; Visitor Center/Office: 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM daily (Colorado Parks and Wildlife)
attractions.admission: Daily Vehicle Pass: $10 - $12 (Colorado license plates), $15 - $17 (Out-of-state license plates); Individual Daily Pass: $4 (for entry by foot, bicycle, or horseback) (Colorado Parks and Wildlife)
highlights: Panoramic views of Horsetooth Reservoir and the city of Fort Collins from the 6,780-foot summit (Colorado Parks and Wildlife).
Moderate to difficult 3.4-mile round-trip hike with approximately 1,100 feet of elevation gain (Day Hikes Near Denver).
Diverse ecological transition through mountain shrublands, meadows, and ponderosa pine forests (COTREX).
Unique bouldering and traditional rock climbing opportunities on the granite formations near the summit (Lory State Park Brochure).
Natural rock stairway providing an adventurous final ascent to the summit peak (Visit Fort Collins).

Located 65 miles west of Fort Collins, it serves as a scenic gateway between the Poudre Canyon and North Park, offering a pristine subalpine environment for year-round recreation. The area is particularly renowned for its spectacular mountain vistas, diverse wildlife, and extensive backcountry trails for hiking and skiing. Wikipedia, US Forest Service

Moose are common and can be aggressive; maintain a safe distance and never approach them. US Forest Service

attractions.name: Kingfisher Point Natural Area (City of Fort Collins)

This site features approximately 147 acres of restored habitats, including riparian cottonwood forests and ponds that provide sanctuary for waterfowl and other wildlife (Public Lands History Center). The area is also home to the Nix Farm Natural Areas Facility and a native plant demonstration garden (City of Fort Collins).

Native Plant Demonstration Garden: Surrounds the office at Nix Farm and is open for self-guided tours (City of Fort Collins).
Pond Activities: Ponds on the west side of Timberline Road are open for fishing and non-motorized boating (City of Fort Collins).
Historic Site: Home to the Great Western Sugar Beet Flume and Bridge (Cache la Poudre River National Heritage Area).
attractions.tips: Use the dedicated parking lot on Timberline Road as there is no trailhead parking available at the Nix Farm office (City of Fort Collins). It features a mix of dense forest, open meadows, and rugged rock outcroppings, offering spectacular views of the Front Range and Horsetooth Reservoir. The trail is popular for technical mountain biking, hiking, and equestrian use, providing a strenuous ascent or a thrilling descent.

attractions.name: Old Town Square
attractions.description: Old Town Square is a historic public plaza in the heart of Downtown Fort Collins, known for its well-preserved architecture, vibrant gathering spaces, and cultural events. Originally constructed in 1985 and significantly renovated in 2015, the square features a state-of-the-art performance stage, interactive splash pads, and numerous outdoor seating areas surrounded by unique shops, galleries, and restaurants. It is a central hub for community life, hosting free concerts, festivals, and year-round activities that exemplify the "quintessential Fort Collins experience."

The square is very pedestrian and bike-friendly, making it a great place to explore on foot (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Poudre Canyon (Cache la Poudre River Canyon)
attractions.description: The Poudre Canyon is a stunning 40-mile glacier-formed valley featuring majestic cliffs and rugged rock formations carved by Colorado’s only nationally designated 'Wild & Scenic' River. It is a premier destination for year-round outdoor recreation, including world-class whitewater rafting, fly fishing, and scenic hiking through the Roosevelt National Forest.
attractions.address: 5216 Poudre Canyon Road (for Gateway Natural Area entrance) / State Highway 14
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Access via Highway 14); Gateway Natural Area: Dawn to Dusk daily (poudreheritage.org)

Cache la Poudre-North Park Scenic and Historic Byway (visitftcollins.com)
World-class whitewater rafting (Class I-V rapids) (visitftcollins.com)

Diverse wildlife viewing including bighorn sheep (colorado.com)
Extensive trail networks including Greyrock and Young Gulch (visitftcollins.com)
attractions.tips: Keep an eye out for bighorn sheep on the rocky cliffs. There is limited cell service in the canyon, so plan ahead. Check river levels for rafting and special fishing regulations in certain sections. (colorado.com, wikipedia.org)
attractions.nearby: Horsetooth Reservoir, Rocky Mountain National Park, State Forest State Park, City of Fort Collins (visitftcollins.com, colorado.com)

attractions.description: The Poudre River Trail is a premier paved recreational pathway that meanders along the scenic Cache la Poudre River for over 45 miles, connecting Bellvue, Fort Collins, Timnath, Windsor, and Greeley. It offers a tranquil escape for walkers, cyclists, and runners, featuring beautiful views of cottonwood groves, wetland meadows, and local wildlife. (Poudre River Trail Corridor, Larimer County)

Scenic views of the Cache la Poudre River and its diverse wildlife (Poudre River Trail Corridor)

The Fort Collins taproom features a cozy, traditional German Rathskeller atmosphere with long communal tables, a dog-friendly interior and exterior, and a spacious outdoor patio. Guests can enjoy a variety of award-winning biers brewed following the Reinheitsgebot purity law using ingredients imported directly from Germany.

Diverse wildlife viewing including elk, mule deer, and pronghorn (Uncover Colorado)
